coverage.notes: Coverage is normalized against the committed cgame corpus instead of the curated function list itself. This pass resolves the remaining retail stats/join-game overlay seam and promotes the close-only command-capture sibling.

functions.comment: Deliberate synthetic retail flag-item predicate beneath `CG_TouchItem`. HLIL returns true only when the supplied `bg_itemlist` row is an `IT_TEAM` item whose tag is one of the first two colored flag slots, excluding the neutral-flag case; the only committed caller uses that gate to suppress the local predicted-pickup path for the red and blue CTF flags.

Deliberate synthetic retail warmup/countdown predicate. HLIL is a compact lookup keyed by gametype that returns true only for Clan Arena, Freeze Tag, Attack and Defend, and Red Rover; committed callers reuse it to gate round-family warmup countdown sounds, start banners, and round transition status prompts.

Deliberate synthetic retail gametype predicate for team-result handling. HLIL is the exact `gametype >= GT_TEAM && gametype != GT_RED_ROVER` test; committed callers use it in the endgame winner-text seam and the `EV_GAMEOVER` music/result branch to choose between team-winner and individual-winner handling.

Retail gametype abbreviation helper used by the match-details ownerdraw seam. HLIL dispatches the active gametype through the same 13-entry short-label table as `cg_newdraw.c::CG_GameTypeShortString`, returning `FFA`, `DUEL`, `RACE`, `TDM`, `CA`, `CTF`, `1F`, `OB`, `HAR`, `FT`, `DOM`, `AD`, or `RR`.

Deliberate synthetic retail helper for Domination point-letter labels. HLIL indexes a fixed `ABCDE?` table, returns `A` through `E` for point indices 1..5, and falls back to `?`; committed callers use it in the `Contesting %s`, `Defending %s`, `Capturing %s`, and `%s captured point %s!` notification paths.

Retail shared `bg_itemlist` lookup keyed by item type and tag, matching bg_misc.c::BG_FindItemByTypeAndTag. HLIL walks the retail item table, returns the first row whose type/tag pair matches the requested values, and otherwise raises the generic `Couldn't find item for type %i tag %i` error; committed callers reuse it for holdables, persistant powerups, and team-item style lookups.

Retail shared powerup-item lookup used by HUD, event, and scoreboard seams. HLIL preserves the `BG_FindItemForPowerup: couldn't find ...` error path, the `PW_INVULNERABILITY -> BG_FindItemForHoldable(HI_INVULNERABILITY)` special case, the persistant-powerup scan for Scout/Guard/Doubler/AmmoRegen, and the final `IT_POWERUP` / `IT_TEAM` search over `bg_itemlist`.

Deliberate synthetic coarse time formatter used by the retail match-details/status ownerdraw seam. HLIL takes the absolute millisecond delta, emits `1s` as the minimum magnitude when the value is smaller than one second, otherwise rounds to whole seconds through `va("%s%1.0fs")`, and prepends the negative sign only when the input is below zero.

Deliberate synthetic shared formatter returning `[-]m:ss.mmm` from a millisecond delta. HLIL takes the absolute value, prepends `-` when the input is negative, preserves the leading-zero seconds field, and emits the final string through `va`, with callsites across the freeze-demo overlay and the race status/times ownerdraw corridor.

Retail shared item-touch proximity test. HLIL evaluates `item->pos` through `BG_EvaluateTrajectory`, preserves the same +/-36 XY bounds, keeps the lowered `-50` floor tolerance, and applies the same enlarged upward Z window for team-flag items before reporting whether the player is close enough to touch the item.

HLIL branches on `item->quantity` through the same 100/50/25 armor cases as `bg_misc.c`, reads the player armor and armor-tier state from `ps->stats`, and preserves the same overstack thresholds for shard, yellow, and red armor.

Deliberate synthetic retail helper at the top of `PmoveSingle`. Retail pmove event-queue append helper; HLIL writes the new event into the alternating event slot with an event parm of 0, clears the paired parm field, and increments the sequence exactly like `PM_AddEvent`; executable fixtures pin the queue wrap.

Retail touch-entity accumulator beneath slide/ground tracing; the body skips `ENTITYNUM_WORLD`, deduplicates the `MAXTOUCH` touch list, and caps overflow before appending the new entity number, with executable fixtures pinning the world, duplicate, and cap gates.

Retail torso-animation setter used by the weapon and gesture paths; HLIL preserves the same `pm_type < PM_DEAD` gate and torsoAnim toggle-bit replacement as `PM_StartTorsoAnim`, with executable fixtures pinning the PM_DEAD no-op and toggle behavior.

Post-weapon torso idle helper reached from `PmoveSingle`; the ready-state branch restores `TORSO_STAND2` for the gauntlet and `TORSO_STAND` for other weapons, while the inherited `PM_ContinueTorsoAnim` torso timer / PM_DEAD suppression gates are pinned by executable fixtures.

Retail legs-animation continuation helper; the body only updates the low animation bits when the current anim differs and `legsTimer` is idle, with executable fixtures pinning both no-op gates before `PM_StartLegsAnim`.
Retail force-legs wrapper that clears `legsTimer` and immediately pushes the new animation for live pm_types, with executable fixtures pinning the timer clear before the PM_DEAD-aware start gate.

functions.signature: void PM_ClipVelocity(vec3_t in, vec3_t normal, vec3_t out, float overbounce)
functions.comment: The body is the standard Quake clip helper: dot the input against the plane normal, choose the overbounce branch, and subtract the scaled normal from the input velocity, which makes `PM_ClipVelocity` exact; executable fixtures pin both plane branches.

Retail friction path beneath every major move mode; HLIL preserves the same speed/drop computation, crouch-slide and diagonal circle-strafe friction overrides, walking-water-special-case gates, water/flight/spectator drops, PM_FREEZE or PMF_SCOREBOARD 0.25 drop multiplier, and final velocity scaling as `PM_Friction`.

functions.signature: static void PM_Accelerate(vec3_t wishdir, float wishspeed, float accel)
functions.comment: Retail acceleration helper shared across walk, air, water, ladder, fly, and noclip movement; the body computes current speed along `wishdir`, clamps `addspeed`, and accumulates the scaled wish vector exactly like `PM_Accelerate`; executable fixtures pin the clamp and overspeed no-op.

Retail command-scaling leaf; HLIL takes the max absolute move component and divides the player speed budget by the command vector length exactly like `PM_CmdScale`, with executable fixtures pinning zero, axial, 2D diagonal, and 3D diagonal inputs.

Deliberate synthetic retail helper shared by the fly, water, ladder, and noclip paths. HLIL applies the active command scale to the current forward/right/up basis, adds the vertical input component, normalizes the resulting 3D wish vector in place, and returns the derived wishspeed.

Retail strafe-direction enumerator for `ps->movementDir`; the branch table maps the forward/right command sign combinations onto the same 0..7 ring used by `PM_SetMovementDir`, and executable fixtures pin the idle side-strafe snaps back to diagonal poses.

Descriptive retail-only jump takeoff body shared by PM_CheckJump and the step-jump seam. HLIL matches the qagame 0x1002E2C0 body: it latches jump flags, derives final vertical takeoff velocity from the active jump-mode globals, uses mode 1's gradient chain-jump scaler, mode 2's additive chain/step branch, or PMF_AIR_CONTROL's additive branch with the post-offset fade; the step-jump latches select `pmove_StepJumpVelocity`, normal jumps select `pmove_ChainJumpVelocity`, and the result is ramp-accumulated then max-clamped before ground state is cleared, EV_JUMP is queued, the forward/back jump animation is selected, and the jump timestamp is refreshed.

Descriptive retail-only crouch step-jump gate checked from the PM_StepSlideMove seam. HLIL matches the qagame 0x1002E4C0 body: it requires the ducked movement flag, rejects the pending step-jump suppression latch, keeps only non-descending vertical velocity, and uses the `cmd.serverTime - jumpTime >= jumpTimeDeltaMin` delay check before reporting success without requiring queued upmove or a fresh jump-release state.

Descriptive retail-only step-jump precheck beneath PM_StepSlideMove. HLIL matches the qagame 0x1002E510 body: it enforces the retail upmove, `cmd.serverTime - jumpTime >= jumpTimeDeltaMin`, and jump-held restrictions around the step-jump modes, with the held-input bypass driven by the compact autoHop slot only; bunnyHop does not suppress the release gate. Rejected general attempts clear queued jump input before the surrounding movement code decides whether to run the general takeoff or fall back to the crouch-step branch.

Retail jump gate beneath walk and air movement; HLIL validates the same jump timers and held-button state, uses only the compact autoHop slot to bypass the held-release gate, routes success through the shared jump launcher, and preserves the grapple pull side path from `PM_CheckJump`.

This is the full retail slide solver from `bg_slidemove.c`: optional gravity half-step, touch-entity accumulation, clip-plane iteration, crease handling, and blocked return code all line up cleanly with `PM_SlideMove`.

Retail stepped movement wrapper over `PM_SlideMove`; HLIL preserves the one-argument gravity boundary, up-trace/down-trace retry, the `"%i:bend can't step\n"` diagnostic, final velocity/origin selection, general PM_CanStepJump recheck and normal step-jump takeoff latch, plus the separate PM_CanCrouchStepJump and shrunken-box clearance fallback before the crouch-step takeoff latch.

Retail forward water-jump probe; HLIL traces 30 units ahead at head level, checks the lip clearance above it, then seeds the same launch velocity, `PMF_TIME_WATERJUMP`, and `pm_time` values as `PM_CheckWaterJump`; executable fixtures now pin the solid-lip and clearance gates.

Retail water-jump continuation helper; it does a gravity-enabled `PM_StepSlideMove`, subtracts gravity from vertical velocity, and clears the water-jump timer as soon as the player starts falling, matching `PM_WaterJumpMove`.

Retail swimming movement path. The body starts with PM_CheckWaterJump, otherwise applies PM_Friction, builds the 3D command vector through PM_BuildWishMove3D, sinks with -60 when idle, clamps to the swim-speed scale, accelerates with PM_Accelerate and pm_wateraccelerate, clips against the ground plane underwater, and finishes with PM_SlideMove(qfalse), which makes PM_WaterMove exact; the idle sink path is fixture-validated.

Retail spectator/flight move leaf reached from PM_SPECTATOR and flight-powerup dispatch; HLIL runs PM_Friction, builds the 3D wish vector through PM_BuildWishMove3D, accelerates with PM_Accelerate and pm_flyaccelerate, and ends in PM_StepSlideMove(qfalse), matching PM_FlyMove without a flight-thrust override.

Retail-expanded invulnerability move handler reached from the walk and air movement tails after PM_Weapon arms PMF_USE_ITEM_HELD for the invulnerability holdable. HLIL gates the active path with the exact `stats[STAT_PLAYER_ITEM_RECHARGE] != 0 && stats[STAT_PLAYER_ITEM_TIME] == 0` stale-timer predicate at playerState +0xf0/+0xec, seeds the active invulnerability powerup state, applies the pitch-driven float / sink bias, counts down the internal activation timer, clears the pending holdable slot only after timer collapse when the +0xf0 recharge rate is zero, and forces the special legs-animation branch while active, matching the reconstructed PM_InvulnerabilityMove boundary.

functions.signature: static void PM_AirControl(vec3_t wishdir, float wishspeed)
functions.comment: Retail air-control redirection leaf beneath `PM_AirMove`. The body strips and restores vertical speed, normalizes the planar velocity, computes the `32 * airControl * dot^2 * frametime` steering term, and reapplies the preserved speed exactly like `PM_AirControl`.

Retail airborne movement path; the committed body runs PM_Friction, PM_SetMovementDir, 2D wishdir normalization, the air-control acceleration mode split for airstop versus strafe movement, the pm_wishspeed clamp on pure side movement, PM_Accelerate as the generic velocity pusher, optional PM_AirControl steering, ground-plane clipping, PM_StepSlideMove(qtrue), PM_InvulnerabilityMove, and the trailing PMF_DOUBLE_JUMP recheck from PM_AirMove.

Retail grapple-pull move. HLIL subtracts the player's origin from `grapplePoint - 16 * forward`, normalizes and scales the pull vector, writes it into `ps->velocity`, and clears the ground-plane latch exactly like `PM_GrappleMove`.

Retail ladder-movement path gated by the ladder probe. The body applies PM_Friction, builds 3D wishvel through PM_BuildWishMove3D, overrides explicit up/down input to +/-0.66 * speed, caps total wishspeed to the same 66% limit, accelerates with PM_Accelerate and pm_accelerate, clips against the ground plane when needed, and ends in PM_SlideMove(qfalse), which matches PM_LadderMove rather than the nearby water move; fixtures pin the vertical clamp.

Retail ladder probe between `PM_DropTimers` and the movement dispatch. HLIL normalizes the flattened forward vector, traces one step ahead with `MASK_PLAYERSOLID`, and raises the ladder latch when the surface flag matches, which is exactly `PM_CheckLadder`; the trace mask and surface gate are fixture-validated.

Retail grounded walk path; HLIL preserves the early swim handoff, PM_ShouldUseInvulnerabilityMove handoff through PM_StepSlideMove(qfalse) and PM_InvulnerabilityMove, jump handoff, friction, crouch-slide timer refresh, ground-plane projection, duck/wade clamps including the crouch-slide 0.75 duck scale, slick/knockback acceleration split, and final PM_StepSlideMove(qfalse) from PM_WalkMove.
Retail corpse-friction helper called only for `PM_DEAD`; the body exits when not walking, subtracts 20 units of speed, and either clears or rescales velocity exactly like `PM_DeadMove`.

Retail noclip path reached from the pm_type dispatch; HLIL forces DEFAULT_VIEWHEIGHT, applies the 1.5x friction variant, rebuilds the 3D wish vector through PM_BuildWishMove3D, accelerates with PM_Accelerate and pm_accelerate, and directly integrates origin by frametime * velocity, which makes PM_NoclipMove exact.

Retail footstep-event selector. The helper honors the pmove noFootsteps gate, then inspects the current ground surface flags and returns the same no-step, metal, snow, wood, and default step event ids used by `PM_FootstepForSurface`.

Retail landing classifier beneath `PM_GroundTrace`; HLIL drives the same land animations, computes the same fall-delta severity, applies the gib-health fall-event gate, clears the PMF_DOUBLE_JUMP latch, and emits the short/medium/far footstep or fall events through the shared pmove event queue, matching `PM_CrashLand`.

Retail allsolid recovery helper under `PM_GroundTrace`; the body emits the exact `"%i:allsolid\n"` diagnostic, jitters the player origin across the same 3x3x3 neighborhood, rebuilds pml.groundTrace with the 0.25-unit down trace on success, and otherwise clears groundEntityNum before dropping pml.groundPlane and pml.walking. No replicated ground-trace history or latest-normal cache is present in playerState.

Retail lift-off helper used by `PM_GroundTrace`; HLIL emits the same `"%i:lift\n"` debug path, traces 64 units down before picking LEGS_JUMP or LEGS_JUMPB/PMF_BACKWARDS_JUMP from the current command, and clears groundEntityNum, pml.groundPlane, and pml.walking without touching a replicated ground-trace cache.

Retail ground-state resolver. The committed body preserves the 0.25-unit trace into pml.groundTrace, allsolid fallback, lift miss, kickoff dot > 10 branch, steep-slope pml.groundPlane-without-walking state, PMF_TIME_WATERJUMP/PMF_TIME_LAND timer clearing, landing handoff into `PM_CrashLand`, -200 z-velocity PMF_TIME_LAND/250 ms latch, the single groundEntityNum playerState store, and final PM_AddTouchEnt, with no replicated ground-trace history side cache.

Retail three-sample water-state helper; HLIL samples MASK_WATER at MINS_Z + 1, halfway from MINS_Z to viewheight, and head height at viewheight, updates `watertype`, and promotes `waterlevel` through the same 0/1/2/3 ladder as `PM_SetWaterLevel`.

Retail leg-animation and footstep-event driver reached after weapon/torso handling; HLIL owns the same crouch-slide branch, airborne LEGS_IDLECR invulnerability idle, LEGS_SWIM water path, idle xyspeed < 5 bobCycle reset, ducked footstep suppression, walk/run axis-speed gate, and bob-boundary footstep, EV_FOOTSPLASH, or EV_SWIM event split as `PM_Footsteps`.

Retail water-transition event helper. The body compares previous and current `waterlevel`, emits the same touch/leave events, suppresses head-water under/clear transitions while PM_DEAD, and skips EV_WATER_CLEAR while invulnerability is active.

Retail begin-change leaf beneath `PM_Weapon`; HLIL validates the requested weapon against the owned-weapon bitmask, queues the change event, stages the drop animation, arms `WEAPON_DROPPING`, and preserves the no-weapon sentinel branch exactly like `PM_BeginWeaponChange`.

Retail finish-change leaf that validates the requested weapon, commits it to `ps->weapon`, moves to `WEAPON_RAISING`, primes `weaponTime`, clears the retail +0xe0 chaingun spin accumulator now named STAT_CHAINGUN_SPINUP, and updates the torso anim, matching `PM_FinishWeaponChange`.

Retail weapon state machine beneath `PmoveSingle`; HLIL preserves respawn/spectator/health gates, holdable use, attack-lockout and PMF_WEAPON_RESET cleanup, weapon-change and sentinel handling, ready/drop/raise states, no-ammo event, fire event, ammo decrement, Scout/AmmoRegen/Haste refire scaling, gauntlet/lightning timing, and the WP_CHAINGUN +0xe0 spin accumulator now named STAT_CHAINGUN_SPINUP.

Retail gesture/taunt animation helper reached after movement and before water events; the body consumes the same button latches, gives `BUTTON_GESTURE` priority, emits `EV_TAUNT` only on that gesture branch, starts voice-command torso animations with the 600 ms timer, and leaves active torso timers untouched.

Retail pmove timer decrement helper; HLIL subtracts `pml.msec` from `pm_time`, `legsTimer`, `torsoTimer`, and the crouch-slide timer, clears `PMF_ALL_TIMES` only when the misc timer expires, and matches the floor-at-zero behavior now pinned by executable fixtures.

Retail viewangle updater; the body skips intermission and dead-player updates while preserving the PM_FREEZE dead-player exception, applies the cmd angle deltas onto `delta_angles`, clamps pitch, and rebuilds the float `viewangles` vector exactly like `PM_UpdateViewAngles`.

This is the full retail per-command pmove dispatcher: it consumes the movement-profile flags already present in playerState, seeds globals, removes body collision for dead players only when PW_INVULNERABILITY is inactive, clamps `msec`, honors PMF_NO_MOVE after updating commandTime, copies previous origin/velocity, updates viewangles, mirrors weaponPrimary/fov and signed command bytes at playerState +0x1dc/+0x1dd/+0x1de, runs the pm_type early exits, dispatches into the recovered ground/water/ladder/fly/grapple/walk/air chain, recharges the progress-backed holdable timer from stats[12] toward stats[10] when PMF_USE_ITEM_HELD is clear, then runs weapon, torso, footstep, and water-event handling.

Shotgun brass-ejection callback installed by `CG_RegisterWeapon`; HLIL preserves the two-shell loop, the mirrored side velocity split, the `(8,0,24)` muzzle offset projected through `cent->lerpAngles`, and the same tumbling fragment setup as src/code/cgame/cg_weapons.c::CG_ShotgunEjectBrass.

Nailgun ejection callback that converts the `(0,-12,24)` muzzle offset through `cent->lerpAngles`, spawns the upward smoke puff at that origin, and then re-tags the local entity as the optimized scale-fade effect exactly like src/code/cgame/cg_weapons.c::CG_NailgunEjectBrass.

Projectile trail callback assigned to rockets and reused by the grenade wrapper. The body evaluates the current and previous projectile trajectory, skips stationary missiles, handles the water bubble-trail split, and emits 50 ms smoke-puff steps using the weapon trail radius and lifetime exactly like src/code/cgame/cg_weapons.c::CG_RocketTrail.
Grenade and proximity-mine trail wrapper installed by `CG_RegisterWeapon`; retail implements it as the same one-line tailcall into `CG_RocketTrail` as src/code/cgame/cg_weapons.c::CG_GrenadeTrail.
Nail projectile trail callback that mirrors the rocket-trail trajectory and water checks but emits the nail puff shader on the same 50 ms cadence, matching src/code/cgame/cg_weapons.c::CG_NailTrail.

Sprite-based plasma trail callback that honors the `cg_noProjectileTrail` and `cg_oldPlasma` gates, allocates a tumbling local entity at the `(2,2,2)` muzzle offset, scales the randomized velocity for water, and colors the sprite from `wi->flashDlightColor` exactly like src/code/cgame/cg_weapons.c::CG_PlasmaTrail.

Grapple chain trail callback installed for the hook weapon. HLIL keeps the same trajectory evaluation, `otherEntityNum` anchor offset by default viewheight/forward/up, the short-distance early-out, and the grappling-chain versus lightning-shader fallback render path from src/code/cgame/cg_weapons.c::CG_GrappleTrail.

functions.signature: static qboolean CG_ResolveClientWeaponColor(const clientInfo_t *ci, byte *rgbOut, vec3_t colorOut)

Deliberate synthetic byte-only sibling of `CG_ResolveClientWeaponColor` used by the retail rail local-entity setup. The body preserves the same local-versus-team/enemy override gates and materializes the packed RGB form needed by the rail sub-entity color fields without the extra float outputs.

HLIL shows this helper allocating one rail-ring local entity, seeding the ring shader, origin, and movement direction, and then tinting the ring through the shared client weapon-color helper before the outer beam loop continues.

functions.signature: void CG_RailTrail(clientInfo_t *ci, vec3_t start, vec3_t end)
functions.comment: Rail trail local-entity builder that allocates the core beam, colors it from the client weapon colors, and optionally spawns the same rail-ring sprite chain as `cg_weapons.c::CG_RailTrail`.

Weapon-media registration path anchored by the retail Couldn't find weapon %i diagnostic; the body resolves the weapon info table entry and registers the same model, icon, and effect media family as src/code/cgame/cg_weapons.c::CG_RegisterWeapon.

Item-media registration path anchored by the out-of-range diagnostic; the body short-circuits already-registered items, registers the world model and icon, and cascades into CG_RegisterWeapon for weapon items.

Exact torso-to-weapon animation mapper from `cg_weapons.c::CG_MapTorsoToWeaponFrame`. HLIL keeps the same three frame windows: `TORSO_DROP` maps to slots `6..14`, `TORSO_ATTACK` maps to `1..6`, `TORSO_ATTACK2` maps to `1..6`, and all other frames fall through to zero.

functions.signature: static void CG_CalculateWeaponPosition(vec3_t origin, vec3_t angles)

HLIL copies `cg.refdef.vieworg` and `cg.refdefViewAngles`, applies the same odd-bob roll/yaw/pitch drift, the same landing-deflect envelope, and the same idle `sin(cg.time * 0.001)` sway as src/code/cgame/cg_weapons.c::CG_CalculateWeaponPosition.

Lightning beam renderer that preserves the true-lightning / stored-prediction split, traces the end point, submits the RT_LIGHTNING beam, and adds the same impact flare as `cg_weapons.c::CG_LightningBolt`.

Railgun flash helper beneath `CG_AddPlayerWeapon`. HLIL gates on `WP_RAILGUN` plus the cached `pe.railgunFlash` latch, clears that latch, copies the muzzle-tag origin and cached impact point, and then forwards the beam to `CG_RailTrail`, matching the current `cg_weapons.c::CG_SpawnRailTrail` reconstruction.

Weapon-barrel spin helper under `CG_AddPlayerWeapon`. The body preserves the `SPIN_SPEED` / `COAST_TIME` timing, toggles the cached spin state on `EF_FIRING` transitions, updates the barrel angle/time fields, and plays the chaingun wind-down sound when the spinning state falls, matching `cg_weapons.c::CG_MachinegunSpinAngle`.

Shared weapon-overlay helper beneath `CG_AddPlayerWeapon`. HLIL reads the owning `centity_t` powerup mask, applies invisibility first, otherwise submits the base entity and conditionally re-submits it through the battlesuit and quad weapon shaders, matching the current `cg_weapons.c::CG_AddWeaponWithPowerups` split.

functions.signature: void CG_AddPlayerWeapon(refEntity_t *parent, playerState_t *ps, centity_t *cent, int team)

First-person view-weapon builder that applies the spectator/intermission/third-person/test-gun guards, computes the viewmodel origin from the current view axes plus FOV drop, maps torso animation frames onto the hand model, and then routes the finished hand entity through `CG_AddPlayerWeapon`, matching `cg_weapons.c::CG_AddViewWeapon`.

Weapon-selection HUD drawer that fades from cg.weaponSelectTime, clears the pickup item display, paints the owned weapon icons plus the selection/no-ammo overlays, and prints the selected pickup name like `cg_weapons.c::CG_DrawWeaponSelect`.

Deliberate synthetic retail name for the menu-HUD weapon-select wrapper. HLIL first applies the shared `cg.weaponSelectTime` and `cg_weaponBar` guards, falls back to `CG_DrawWeaponSelect` for the legacy styles, and in the `cg_weaponBar == 4` path draws the centered 32x32 weapon strip, selection frame, no-ammo overlay, and selected pickup name using the same playerstate weapon bits as the classic selector.

Retail shared `weapnext` / `weapprev` helper. HLIL owns the local `cg.snap` / follow guard, updates `cg.weaponSelectTime`, walks the weapon cursor forward or backward based on the boolean argument, skips both `WP_GAUNTLET` and the `WP_NUM_WEAPONS` sentinel slot, and commits the first selectable weapon through `CG_SetWeaponSelect`, matching the new source-side helper split in `cg_weapons.c`.
Retail `weapnext` command handler for cycling the selected weapon, matching CG_NextWeapon_f.
Retail `weapprev` command handler for cycling the selected weapon backward, matching CG_PrevWeapon_f.
Retail `weapon` command handler that keeps the public-source selection path and also accepts the retail `toggle` argument variant.

Retail highest-slot fallback beneath the `EV_NOAMMO` seam. The body refreshes `cg.weaponSelectTime`, walks the owned/selectable weapon slots from high to low while skipping the `WP_NUM_WEAPONS` sentinel, and commits the first valid result through `CG_SetWeaponSelect`, matching the current `cg_weapons.c::CG_SelectHighestWeapon` helper.

Retail sibling of `CG_SelectHighestWeapon` used by the unreconstructed `EV_DROP_WEAPON` seam in HLIL. The helper is identical to the highest-slot fallback except that it skips the weapon id passed through the second register argument before selecting the highest remaining owned/selectable slot.

EV_FIRE_WEAPON handler anchored by the retail weapon-range diagnostic; the body stamps muzzleFlashTime, handles predicted local lightning, plays quad and flash sounds, and runs brass ejection like `cg_weapons.c::CG_FireWeapon`.

functions.signature: void CG_MissileHitWall(int weapon, int clientNum, vec3_t origin, vec3_t dir, impactSound_t soundType)

functions.signature: void CG_MissileHitPlayer(int weapon, vec3_t origin, vec3_t dir, int entityNum)

functions.signature: static void CG_MissileHitWallDmgThrough(vec3_t origin, vec3_t dir, int weapon)

EV_SHOTGUN handler that emits the Rage Pro smoke-puff guard and then drives the shared pellet-pattern replay, matching `cg_weapons.c::CG_ShotgunFire`.

Deliberate synthetic retail-only shotgun finisher effect reached from `EV_SHOTGUN_KILL`. HLIL divides the event payload into repeated gore samples, jitters each sample around the victim origin, emits paired `CG_Bleed` spurts, and conditionally spawns the same translucent spray puff used by the retail blood path.

Local `q_math` normal-table lookup compiled into `cgamex86.dll`. HLIL preserves the exact `NUMVERTEXNORMALS` (`0xA2`) upper-bound check, falls back to `vec3_origin` for invalid indices, and otherwise copies the three-float row directly from `bytedirs[b]`, matching `q_math.c::ByteToDir`.

functions.signature: void RotatePointAroundVector(vec3_t dst, const vec3_t dir, const vec3_t point, float degrees)

Local `q_math` axis-rotation helper compiled into `cgamex86.dll`. The body rebuilds `axis[1]` with `PerpendicularVector`, optionally rotates it around `axis[0]` through `RotatePointAroundVector`, and then recomputes `axis[2]` via the cross product, matching `q_math.c::RotateAroundDirection`.

functions.signature: void vectoangles(const vec3_t value1, vec3_t angles)
functions.comment: Local vector-to-Euler conversion helper compiled into `cgamex86.dll`. HLIL preserves the zero-forward special case, the `atan2` yaw/pitch calculation, the 0..360 wrapping, and the final `angles[PITCH] = -pitch; angles[YAW] = yaw; angles[ROLL] = 0;` layout from `q_math.c::vectoangles`.

functions.signature: void AnglesToAxis(const vec3_t angles, vec3_t axis[3])
functions.comment: Local Euler-to-axis helper compiled into `cgamex86.dll`. HLIL calls the already-mapped `AngleVectors` leaf to fill `axis[0]` and `axis[2]`, then negates the temporary right vector into `axis[1]`, exactly matching `q_math.c::AnglesToAxis`.
Local axis-matrix copy helper compiled into `cgamex86.dll`. The body is a straight nine-float copy from `in[0..2][0..2]` to `out[0..2][0..2]`, matching `q_math.c::AxisCopy` exactly.

functions.signature: void ProjectPointOnPlane(vec3_t dst, const vec3_t p, const vec3_t normal)
functions.comment: Local plane-projection helper compiled into `cgamex86.dll`. The body computes `inv_denom = 1 / DotProduct(normal, normal)`, projects `p` onto the plane defined by `normal`, and writes the result to `dst`, matching `q_math.c::ProjectPointOnPlane`.

functions.signature: float LerpAngle(float from, float to, float frac)

Local wrapped-angle subtraction helper compiled into `cgamex86.dll`. The loop structure matches the public `q_math.c::AngleSubtract` behavior of folding the difference back into the [-180, 180] range.

Local in-place vector normalization helper compiled into `cgamex86.dll`. HLIL computes the vector length, returns it, and scales the incoming vector by the inverse length when non-zero, matching `q_math.c::VectorNormalize`.

functions.signature: vec_t VectorNormalize2(const vec3_t v, vec3_t out)

functions.signature: void MatrixMultiply(float in1[3][3], float in2[3][3], float out[3][3])
functions.comment: Local 3x3 matrix multiply helper compiled into `cgamex86.dll`. HLIL performs the same explicit nine-cell multiply-accumulate sequence as `q_math.c::MatrixMultiply`.

functions.signature: void AngleVectors(const vec3_t angles, vec3_t forward, vec3_t right, vec3_t up)

Local parser-text compressor compiled into `cgamex86.dll`. HLIL strips both `//` and `/* */` comments, folds whitespace runs down to a single space or newline, preserves quoted strings verbatim, and returns the compressed byte count, matching `q_shared.c::COM_Compress`.

Local token parser compiled into `cgamex86.dll`. The body resets the shared token buffer, skips whitespace and C-style comments, optionally returns early on line breaks, handles quoted strings, and advances the caller cursor exactly like `q_shared.c::COM_ParseExt`.

Compiled `ui_shared` numeric-string probe. HLIL rejects null or empty input, accepts only digits plus at most one decimal point, and returns failure on any other byte, matching `ui_shared.c::UI_IsNumericValue` exactly; committed callers use it in the preset comparison and preset-application seams.

functions.signature: void Q_strncpyz(char *dest, const char *src, int destsize)

functions.signature: int Q_stricmpn(const char *s1, const char *s2, int n)

functions.signature: void Q_strcat(char *dest, int size, const char *src)

Local printable-length helper compiled into `cgamex86.dll`. The loop counts visible characters while skipping Quake color escapes `^0..^7`, matching `q_shared.c::Q_PrintStrlen`.

Deliberate synthetic shared `m:ss` formatter. HLIL divides the incoming second count into minutes plus tens/ones, then returns `va("%i:%i%i")`; the same helper is reused by the retail classic level timer, centered scoreboard clock, and the already-mapped team time-held ownerdraw path.

Local in-place printable-string scrubber compiled into `cgamex86.dll`. HLIL skips Quake color escapes, copies only printable ASCII bytes into the destination cursor, terminates the cleaned string, and returns the original pointer, matching `q_shared.c::Q_CleanStr`.

functions.signature: int Com_sprintf(char *dest, int size, const char *fmt, ...)
functions.comment: Local bounded formatter compiled into `cgamex86.dll`. The function formats through a 32000-byte stack buffer, throws the same fatal `overflowed bigbuffer` error, prints the same overflow warning, and finishes via `Q_strncpyz`, matching `q_shared.c::Com_sprintf`.
Local temp-buffer varargs formatter compiled into `cgamex86.dll`. HLIL alternates between two 32000-byte static buffers and writes the formatted string with `vsprintf`, matching `q_shared.c::va`.

Local info-string lookup helper compiled into `cgamex86.dll`. HLIL preserves the BIG_INFO overflow guard, the alternating static return buffers, the backslash-delimited key/value scan, and the bounded compare against the requested key, matching `q_shared.c::Info_ValueForKey`.

functions.signature: static void CG_SetScaledPackedRGBA(byte *rgba, unsigned packedColor, int colorScale)

Compiled `ui_shared` memory-pool allocator. HLIL checks `allocPoint + size` against the 0x600000-byte UI pool, raises the exact `UI_Alloc: Failure. Out of memory!` diagnostic when exhausted, returns `&memoryPool[allocPoint]`, and advances the pool cursor with 16-byte alignment, matching `ui_shared.c::UI_Alloc`.

Compiled `ui_shared` string-pool allocator. Compiled `ui_shared` string/parser bootstrap. The function clears the string-hash table and pool counters, resets menu/open-menu counts, runs `UI_InitMemory`, rebuilds both keyword hashes, and conditionally refreshes bindings through `Controls_GetConfig`, which is the exact `ui_shared.c::String_Init` flow.

Compiled `ui_shared` parser error reporter. HLIL formats through a large static buffer, queries `trap_PC_SourceFileAndLine`, and prints the exact red `ERROR: %s, line %d: %s\n` message used by `ui_shared.c::PC_SourceError`.

functions.signature: void LerpColor(vec4_t a, vec4_t b, vec4_t c, float t)

Compiled `ui_shared` char-pointer float reader. HLIL calls the shared `COM_ParseExt` token path with `qfalse`, rejects empty tokens, and writes `atof(token)` into the destination, matching `ui_shared.c::Float_Parse`.

Compiled `ui_shared` PC-token float reader. HLIL uses `trap_PC_ReadToken`, supports a leading `-`, rejects non-number tokens through `PC_SourceError`, and writes either `token.floatvalue` or its negation into the destination, matching `ui_shared.c::PC_Float_Parse`.

Compiled `ui_shared` char-pointer color reader. The helper loops four times through the recovered `Float_Parse` leaf, storing one parsed channel per iteration into the destination `vec4_t`, which is exact `ui_shared.c::Color_Parse`.

Compiled `ui_shared` PC-token color reader. The helper loops four times through the same token-reader path used by `PC_Float_Parse`, storing one channel per iteration into the destination `vec4_t`, which is the exact `ui_shared.c::PC_Color_Parse` contract.

Compiled `ui_shared` char-pointer integer reader. HLIL reuses the same `COM_ParseExt` token path as `Float_Parse`, rejects empty tokens, and publishes `atoi(token)` into the destination, matching `ui_shared.c::Int_Parse`.

Compiled `ui_shared` PC-token integer reader. HLIL mirrors the public helper exactly: read one token, optionally consume a leading `-`, require `TT_NUMBER`, route failures through `PC_SourceError`, and publish `token.intvalue` with the sign applied.

Compiled `ui_shared` char-pointer rectangle reader. The body chains four `Float_Parse` calls into `r->x`, `r->y`, `r->w`, and `r->h`, returning success only when all four token parses succeed, which is exact `ui_shared.c::Rect_Parse`.

Compiled `ui_shared` PC-token string reader. The body reads exactly one token through `trap_PC_ReadToken`, interns `token.string` through the recovered `String_Alloc` helper, and returns success, matching `ui_shared.c::PC_String_Parse`.

Compiled `ui_shared` script-block parser. HLIL zeroes a 1024-byte staging buffer, requires the opening `{`, then reads tokens until `}`, quoting multi-character tokens through `va("\"%s\"")`, appending spaces with `Q_strcat`, and finally interning the assembled script through `String_Alloc`, which is exact `ui_shared.c::PC_Script_Parse`.

Compiled `ui_shared` gradient-bar painter. HLIL sets the renderer color to the supplied vec4, draws `DC->Assets.gradientBar` across the requested rect, and then clears the renderer color, matching `ui_shared.c::GradientBar_Paint` exactly.

Deliberate synthetic retail name for the browser preset-list synchronizer. Deliberate synthetic retail name for the browser child-layout updater. HLIL shows the helper inheriting the parent origin and optional border offset, clearing the cached child layout fields at `+0x108/+0x10C`, and rewriting each child absolute `x/y/w/h` from the parent-relative rectangle data, matching the `Menu_UpdatePosition` and `Item_SetScreenCoords` role split in `ui_shared.c`.

functions.signature: static void *CG_BrowserGetMatchingItemByNumber(const char *name, void *overlay, int index)

Deliberate synthetic retail name for the browser script dispatcher. functions.signature: static qboolean CG_SetBrowserFocus(void *widget, float x, float y)

Deliberate synthetic retail name for the list-control max-scroll helper. It is the exact browser-list equivalent of `ui_shared.c::Item_ListBox_MaxScroll`, deriving the maximum top-row offset from the feeder count callback and the visible element span in the current list orientation.

Deliberate synthetic retail name for the list-control thumb-position helper. It is the exact browser-list equivalent of `ui_shared.c::Item_ListBox_ThumbPosition`, converting the current list start position into a scrollbar thumb origin for both horizontal and vertical browser lists.

Deliberate synthetic retail name for the list-control thumb draw-position helper. Deliberate synthetic retail name for the slider thumb-position helper. It is the exact browser-widget equivalent of `ui_shared.c::Item_Slider_ThumbPosition`: clamp the current bound cvar value to the widget min and max range and convert that normalized value into the live thumb X position relative to the caption offset.

functions.signature: static int CG_BrowserSliderOverControl(void *widget, float x, float y)
functions.comment: Deliberate synthetic retail name for the slider hit-test helper. functions.signature: static int CG_BrowserListOverLB(void *widget, float x, float y)
functions.comment: Deliberate synthetic retail name for the list-control hit-test helper. Deliberate synthetic retail name for the browser mouse-over flag helper. It is the exact browser-widget equivalent of `ui_shared.c::Item_SetMouseOver`, setting or clearing the retail mouse-over window bit on the target widget based on the requested focus state.

functions.signature: static void CG_BrowserMouseEnter(void *widget, float x, float y)
functions.comment: Deliberate synthetic retail name for the browser mouse-enter handler. It is the exact browser-widget equivalent of `ui_shared.c::Item_MouseEnter`: reject hidden controls, refresh the corrected text-rect hover state, run the enter script, set the widget mouse-over bit through `CG_SetBrowserMouseOver`, and seed list-control hover state when the widget is a browser list.

Deliberate synthetic retail name for the browser mouse-leave handler. It is the exact browser-widget equivalent of `ui_shared.c::Item_MouseLeave`: clear the mouse-over text bit, run the exit script, drop list arrow hover flags, and clear the widget hover state through `CG_SetBrowserMouseOver`.

Deliberate synthetic retail name for the browser yes-or-no key handler. Deliberate synthetic retail name for the browser multi-choice index resolver. Deliberate synthetic retail name for the browser multi-choice label resolver. It is the exact browser-widget equivalent of `ui_shared.c::Item_Multi_Setting`, reading the current cvar as either a string or float and returning the matching display label from the widget's retail multi-definition block.

Deliberate synthetic retail name for the browser multi-choice key handler. It is the exact browser-widget equivalent of `ui_shared.c::Item_Multi_HandleKey`: on an activation key it advances the current entry index, wraps at the end of the multi-definition list, and writes the selected string or formatted numeric value back through the bound widget cvar.

Deliberate synthetic retail name for the browser preset-list label resolver. It is the exact browser-widget equivalent of `ui_shared.c::Item_PresetList_Setting`: fetch the current preset cvar string, walk the preset list entries, and fall back to the literal `Custom` label when no list entry matches.

Deliberate synthetic retail name for the browser preset-list index resolver. It is the exact browser-widget equivalent of `ui_shared.c::Item_PresetList_FindCvarByValue`, returning the current preset entry index or `-1` when the selection is still the retail custom state.

Deliberate synthetic retail name for the browser preset-list key handler. It is the exact browser-widget equivalent of `ui_shared.c::Item_PresetList_HandleKey`: on activation it advances the current preset index, resolves the sibling preset-values widget by name, applies each linked cvar from the selected preset row, and then stores the chosen preset label in the bound preset cvar.

functions.signature: static qboolean CG_BrowserTextFieldHandleKey(int key, qboolean down)

Deliberate synthetic retail name for the browser controls-config fetch helper. It is the exact browser-controls equivalent of `ui_shared.c::Controls_GetConfig`, inlining the key-assignment scan across the browser-local bindings table and caching the first two matching keys for each command.

functions.signature: static void CG_SetBrowserFeederSelection(void *overlay, int feeder, int index)
